pmos

/summary-tldr — design contract (epic 0612-h2j)

Created using pmos‑toolkit

Overview

/summary-tldr is a new pmos-toolkit skill. Given any single piece of user-supplied content — an article or web URL, raw pasted text, a PDF, a markdown file, an image, a video URL, an email thread, a tweet or Twitter thread, or a podcast episode — it produces a faithful, grounded summary at a user-confirmed compression target and in a user-chosen output style, runs a first-time-reader review pass, optionally hands the result to /diagram, and saves it as a standalone HTML doc under {docs_path}/summary-tldr/{YYYY-MM-DD}-<slug>.

It sits alongside /polish, /artifact, and /diagram as a content/authoring tool. It is a standalone utility — not a stage in the requirements→spec→plan pipeline, and it does not load workstream context.

The north star is one sentence: a reader who never saw the original should come away with the source's actual claims, numbers, and takeaways — not a description of what the document is about. Every design choice below serves that.

This is a singleton epic (D18): one brand-new skill wrapped as one build story (0612-ejq). Route: skill. This design doc is the cross-cutting contract the story's /plan cites by anchor; there is no separate epic /spec (the skill spec folds into the story plan).

Maintainer decisions captured at define (2026-06-12)

D1 — Plugin = pmos-toolkit (as requested).
Ships in pmos-toolkit as a content/authoring tool beside /polish, /artifact, /diagram. Acknowledged tension: the reusable podcast/video transcription substrate (transcribe.sh) lives in pmos-learnkit's /magazine; cross-plugin reuse for media inputs is accepted (see Input ingestion).
D2 — Compression = research-informed bands + absolute cap.
Three intent-labeled bands (Tight ~10–20%, Standard ~20–30% default, Detailed ~30–40% with a "this will be long" nudge), plus an absolute word cap scaled to source length so long sources still produce a TL;DR. The target is confirmed with the user every run. Rationale: the empirical sweet spot is ~15–30% and compression is non-linear (research notes).
D3 — Inputs = tiered with honest degradation.
v1 fully supports native inputs (raw text, markdown, PDF, image, web/doc URL, pasted email thread, pasted tweet/thread). Podcast/video are best-effort via transcription-if-available; with no transcript path the skill refuses that input with guidance — never fabricates.
D4 — Writing quality = inline the relevant /polish checks.
The applicable /polish rubric checks are inlined into the review pass (concision, de-AI-slop, active voice, no-meta-description), citing polish/reference/rubric.md as the source. /polish is not invoked — a summary has no author voice to preserve, and inlining is subagent-safe.

Invocation

Natural-language-first (§I). Every flag also has a natural-language form; an explicit flag overrides.

/summary-tldr <source> [--compression tight|standard|detailed] [--style bullets|exec|nested|layered]
              [--audience <who>] [--diagram] [--out <path>] [--non-interactive | --interactive]
  • <source> — a URL, a local file path (pdf/md/image/txt), or pasted inline content (raw text, an email thread, a tweet thread). When ambiguous, the skill asks what the input is.
  • --compression (contract flag — typed value) selects the band without the confirm prompt; absent → the skill proposes a band and confirms.
  • --style (contract flag — typed value) selects the output style; absent → the skill asks.
  • --diagram pre-answers the optional diagram step (silent alias for the gate's Run option).
  • --out overrides the default save path.
  • --non-interactive / --interactive per the canonical W14 contract (the gates AUTO-PICK their Recommended option; open questions buffer into the artifact).

Architecture — reuse vs new

Reuse (cite, do not fork)

  • _shared/pipeline-setup.md — settings, {docs_path} resolution.
  • _shared/non-interactive.md — the canonical inline block (byte-identical), enforced by the repo lint.
  • _shared/findings-dispositions.md — review-finding presentation (Fix/Modify/Skip/Defer).
  • _shared/html-authoring/ — the emit checklist (template slot-fill, atomic write, asset copy, kebab heading ids, sections.json, index regen, cache-bust, pmos:skill meta).
  • /polish input resolver pattern (Phase 1): local path → Read; http(s):// → WebFetch; notion:// → Notion MCP; inline text → the argument is the doc.
  • /polish rubric checks (inlined, voice machinery dropped — D4).
  • /magazine media-transcription pattern (cross-plugin): transcribe.sh <enclosure> <guid> --model <whisper-model> with its exit-3 "no whisper → honest hint, never fabricate" contract; article crawl redirected to file, never piped (pipes truncate at 8–64 KB).
  • /diagram main-agent handoff (like /artifact Phase 3.7): /diagram --source <md> --theme editorial --non-interactive --on-failure drop, validate SVG before embed.

New (genuinely this skill's)

  • The input-type dispatcher covering all source kinds with per-kind preprocessing and honest degradation (below).
  • The compression model (intent bands + length-scaled absolute cap).
  • The hybrid extract-then-generate summarization pipeline with map-reduce chunking for long sources.
  • The first-time-reader review pass (FineSurE-style coverage/faithfulness/standalone/asserts-not-describes/coherence gate) — the heart of the skill.
  • The summary-tldr library listing of past summaries.
  • Tweet-thread stitching and email-thread quote-dedup preprocessors.

Input ingestion (tiered, honest degradation)

The dispatcher detects the source kind, preprocesses to clean text, and records a source_kind + extraction_confidence. Best practice across the literature: transcribe/extract to clean text first, then summarize — preprocessing quality gates summary quality.

Source kindv1 handlingDegradation
Raw text / pastedThe argument is the document.—
Markdown fileRead the file.—
Web / document URLWebFetch, strip to text.Fetch fail → report, ask for paste.
PDFBuilt-in Read (native PDF).Scanned/low-text PDF → flag low confidence, summarize what extracted, say so.
ImageBuilt-in vision Read.Low legibility → flag, never invent text.
Email threadPasted; dedup quoted/forwarded text, order chronologically.—
Tweet / Twitter threadPasted or URL; stitch in posting order before summarizing.URL behind auth → ask for paste.
Podcast episodeTranscribe via /magazine transcribe.sh (cross-plugin), then summarize transcript; chunk if long.No whisper / no transcript → refuse with install guidance, never fabricate (exit-3 contract).
Video URLUse an available transcript/captions; else transcribe audio like a podcast.No transcript path → refuse with guidance.

Hard rule: when extraction/transcription confidence is low, the skill flags it and summarizes only what it actually has — it never silently summarizes degraded or absent text.

Compression model (D2)

For text sources, the skill measures source length, proposes a band, and confirms the target with the user (unless --compression sets it). Bands are intent-labeled, not raw-percent-only:

BandApprox. %Use
Tight~10–20%Skim / BLUF; high compression.
Standard (default)~20–30%The empirical sweet spot for most content.
Detailed~30–40%Dense technical/legal/scientific only; carries a "this will be long" nudge.
  • Absolute cap. Because compression is non-linear, the percentage is bounded by a word cap scaled to source length, so a long source still yields a TL;DR rather than a sprawling document. The cap is surfaced to the user alongside the band.
  • Non-text sources (image, short tweet) have no meaningful source-length %, so the skill proposes a target length directly instead of a ratio.

Summarization pipeline (hybrid extract-then-generate)

The grounded pipeline, derived from the faithfulness research:

  1. Preprocess to clean text (transcribe / OCR-read / dedup quotes / stitch threads) — Input ingestion.
  2. Chunk if long — map-reduce (segment → summarize each independently → synthesize) to defeat the lost-in-the-middle positional bias.
  3. Extract a keyfact list from the source (claims, numbers, named conclusions, entities). This is grounding-by-construction.
  4. Generate the summary to cover and assert those keyfacts at the chosen band/style — stating the source's claims directly, never describing the document.
  5. Review (first-time-reader pass) and apply the inlined writing checks.

Output styles

The skill asks for a preferred style (unless --style sets it). All styles front-load the conclusion (inverted pyramid). Offered:

  • Key-takeaways bullets (default) — a BLUF line + bulleted key points; best for articles/blogs.
  • Executive narrative — a coherent prose abstract; best for work/business docs.
  • Nested / hierarchical bullets — best for long, structured docs and transcripts.
  • Layered / progressive — one-liner → bullets → detail; best for very long sources (books, episodes).

Grounding rules (no meta-description)

The user's central constraint, made enforceable:

  • Assert, don't describe. The summary conveys the source's claims/numbers/conclusions. "This article discusses X", "the document explains Y" and similar meta-description are a hard fail in the review pass and must be rewritten to the actual claim.
  • No claim absent from the source. Every sentence traces to source content fetched/extracted this run. Nothing from model memory.
  • Preserve exact numbers, entities, and named conclusions; prefer the source's own framing of its thesis.
  • Long sources: chunk-and-synthesize, not single-pass, to avoid coverage gaps.

Writing quality (inlined /polish checks — D4)

Applied to the drafted summary as a lightweight rubric, citing polish/reference/rubric.md. Borrowed (voice-preservation machinery dropped — a summary has no author voice):

  • Concision: clutter words, hedging stacks, empty transitions, em-dash overuse.
  • De-AI-slop: AI-vocabulary hard-bans + soft-flags, tricolon overuse, "not just X, it's Y" rhetoric.
  • Active voice (passive-ratio), sentence-length variance, header inflation, full-sentence-bullet abuse, vague modifiers.
  • Reframed "throat-clearing intro" check → the opening states the bottom line immediately, no filler (overlaps the no-meta-description rule).

Deterministic checks (regex / script-metric) auto-apply; judgment checks surface per findings-dispositions.md. Arithmetic is done by a script, never the model (§H).

First-time-reader review pass

The quality gate the user asked for — a FineSurE-style check, run before emit, from the perspective of a reader who never saw the original:

  1. Coverage — is every source keyfact present? (matched keyfacts / total)
  2. Faithfulness — does every sentence trace to source? Scan the 7 error types (entity, predicate, circumstance, coreference, discourse-link, grammatical, out-of-context).
  3. Standalone — no dangling references or undefined pronouns for someone with no access to the original.
  4. Asserts, not describes — fail any meta-description; rewrite to the actual claim.
  5. Coherence — reads as organized prose/bullets, not stitched fragments.

A simple coverage/faithfulness signal is surfaced to the user. The pass uses a reviewer sub-step grounded in quoted source spans (≥40-char quotes), with a ≤2-loop remediation cap; residual gaps are surfaced, never hidden.

Optional diagram step

After the summary lands, the skill offers an optional gate (Recommended = Skip): "Convert this summary into a diagram?" On Run (or --diagram), the main agent calls /diagram --source <summary.md> --theme editorial --non-interactive --on-failure drop, validates the returned SVG (parses, dark-mode background rect, heading-id smoke), and embeds it in the summary HTML; on failure it logs and continues without the diagram. /diagram is invoked from the main agent only, never a subagent.

Output artifact & save location

Emit per the _shared/html-authoring/README.md checklist. Deltas:

  • Artifact path: {docs_path}/summary-tldr/{YYYY-MM-DD}-<summary-slug>.html (slug derived from the source title/topic); --out overrides.
  • summary-tldr = summary-tldr.
  • Sidecars: <artifact>.sections.json; the summary carries a sources/provenance block (source kind, URL/filename, extraction confidence, retrieval timestamp).
  • A summary-tldr.html library listing of past summaries is regenerated under {docs_path}/summary-tldr/.

Self-contained, offline-openable from file:// (read mode), with the comments overlay baked in.

Honest degradation

One rule, no third option: never a fabricated summary, never an invented fact, never a silently degraded source. When transcription/OCR/fetch fails or returns low-confidence text, the skill says so plainly and either summarizes only what it has (flagged) or refuses that input with guidance.

Cross-cutting invariants (the contract stories cite)

  • I1 — Grounded, not meta. Output asserts the source's actual content; meta-description is a hard review fail.
  • I2 — Verified-this-run. Every claim traces to source content fetched/extracted this run; nothing from memory.
  • I3 — Confirmed compression. The compression target is proposed and confirmed (or set by flag) before generating; bounded by a length-scaled cap.
  • I4 — First-time-reader gate before emit. Coverage + faithfulness + standalone + asserts-not-describes + coherence, ≤2-loop remediation, residuals surfaced.
  • I5 — Honest degradation. No fabrication; low-confidence/absent extraction is flagged or refused.
  • I6 — Substrate-compliant emit. html-authoring checklist, kebab heading ids, non-interactive inline block byte-identical, skill-patterns §A–§L.

Out of scope (v1)

  • Multi-document synthesis (summarizing several sources into one) — v1 is one source per run.
  • Persistent background transcription queue (that's /magazine watch's job) — v1 transcribes inline, best-effort.
  • Translation; summarizing into a different language than the source.
  • Editing/rewriting the original (that's /polish).
  • Paywalled/auth-gated scraping — respect access controls; ask for a paste instead.
pmos Source: docs/pmos/features/2026-06-12_summary-tldr-skill/02_design.html · Created using pmos‑toolkit
